prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `73c3b0d8`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 task package for `73c3b0d8`. The rule is: every yellow cell drops by one row, and any dropped yellow that lands immediately above the fixed red barrier row expands along both upward diagonals to form a V. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry for this task. No new primitive was needed.

The generator stays close to the task family by sampling one full red row plus sparse, isolated yellow singleton inputs, with at most one explicit V-triggering dot. Verification passed on all official examples, the build produced 1000 examples, generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview sheet after tightening the sampler to prevent adjacent yellow input noise.
